post.content:
Current company - Google
Level - L3
YOE - 2.5
Cuurent TC - 38 LPA

Offer LinkedIn - Software Engineer
Base - 42
Target Bonus - 4.2 (10 % of base)
Stcoks - 100k USD MSFT stocks
PF + Gratuity = 2L + 80k = 2.8L
TC - 70.5LPA

Getting Systems Infra org at LinkedIn, please help me answer some questions if you are from LI or Uber, or know any friend's experience who are in either of these?

1. How is LI's Systems Infra org in terms of growth and wlb?

2. Shall I accept the offer? I also cleared uber SDE2 (offer in progress), however the recruiter said that Uber has stopped giving counter offers, the max they can match is my LI's offer. Also, she said that 100K stocks is something which Uber can't give, she said that probably they will try to increase base, bonus etc!

3. LI's role is SE and not SSE, shall I accept it considering my YOE and growth trajectory? Won't it delay my carrer growth in becoming SSE, and manager ahead?

4. LI's (MSFT) stock is pretty stable and expected to grow in future, however uber stock is very volatile and considering they will be offering significantly less stock (50K-75K at max based on other leetcode offer details), seems like my future savings in Uber will be negligible/negative comapred to staying in LI at a downslevel (since SE is not SDE2 right?)

5. Honestly, I want to work on consumer facing product which i think i will get in Uber and not LinkedIn (since LI is providing me systems infra org, while Uber is offering me something in the mobility org, i asked for consumer facing product team to Uber HR, and hoping to get that). But at the same time, my savings will get a significat setback upon choosing Uber. What shall I do, kind of confused, any logical opinion would be really helpfull in deciding :). Please help!


I am happy answer any of your questions, but need some opinions on the above questions, to help me take the correct decision in return. :)

Thanks folks! cheers!!!

Congratulations!
I would go for Linkedin.

Also, can you share your interview experience?
Do questions were linkedin tagged lc?
And HLD?
and they say there is a recession in the market :D

For 2.5 years LinkedIn wont be able to give SSE position, but i think this is one of the best offers I have seen for SE position, this is very close to many SSE offers which linkedin gives.
I have seen many cases where people got promoted to SSE after 1.5 years post joining Linkedin, so your shouldnt worry about the position.
Also, she said that 100K stocks is something which Uber can't give ---> This statement is correct, uber wont be able to give this much stock, however they can match the ctc my giving more bonus or base component.
WLB is compartively better in Linkedin than uber, also it's not certain that you will be working on something which is direcly customer facing in uber as well.

hii , congrats for the offer , my two cents -1.if possible you should go for uber as it has better brand value and if they can much with base its good na , higher base is better and its isnt bad to get a SE role now as you have 2.5 years of exp only plus you TC is very good but i would say to choose sde 2 of uber over LI SE...                                                                                                                        can you please share your preparation strategy and are you a CPer on codeforces and how to tackle OA (is it fine to take help in OAs) ?
how long did it take you to get the offer?
which tool you used in Linkedin Interview for system design to draw ?\n\n

SE at LinkedIn maps to sde-2ish. Based on feedback from my friends and network the infra org is pretty good. Work load is manageable and in general they work on interesting problems. Uber will be closer to Amazon in terms of engineering culture. As you pointed out, uber being an operations company is likely to have lower margins and potentially slower stock growth. But that\'s an assumption. Based on current data available, I\'ll take LinkedIn if TC is the same. \n\nPS: I\'ve personally not worked in either of the companies
Congratulations!!!Can you please tell me how did you apply for the job , through referral or recruiter reached out to you on linkedin or somewhere else?
Congrats op, that\u2019s a great offer! Did you have any competing offers or this was the initial offer they gave?
Pls share your interview experience as well
Hey could you please say how you got opportunities for linkedin and uber?
